Text:
The last bend of the river opens into a quiet stretch of land. The water runs clear, berry bushes cling to the bank, and a stand of young trees waits just beyond the shore.
It is not much yet, but it is enough.
The beavers gather around the camp, tired but hopeful. Before the work begins, the scouts report one small blessing found nearby.
[[C]]A hidden patch of ripe berries is found nearby. [[+]][[good:Berries:{0:+0}]]
[[C]]Clean water collects in a shallow pool. [[+]][[good:Water:{0:+0}]]
[[C]]Dry fallen trunks lie near the treeline. [[+]][[good:Log:{0:+0}]]
[[C]]A young kit is found near the river, barely alive. [[+]]{0:+0} [[icon:UI/images/game/ico-child]] Child Beaver

{0} has passed away.

The colony grows quiet. For the first time, the beavers are not only thinking about survival, but about memory. A few gather nearby, unsure what to do next. Work still needs doing, but so does grief.

[[C]]All beavers permanently gain the <GreenHighlight>+Remembrance</GreenHighlight> Wellbeing bonus.
[[C]][[-]]{good:Plank:{0}} within [[time:{1}d]]
There is no time for this. The colony must move on. <YellowHighlight>Unknown consequences.</YellowHighlight>

The logs are lashed together into a small raft, just large enough to carry {0} downriver.
The colony watches in silence as the current takes them away. It is a simple farewell, but it gives the beavers a little peace.
[[C]][[+]]All beavers gain the <GreenHighlight>+Comforted</GreenHighlight> Wellbeing bonus for some time.

The planks are shaped into a small cemetery marker for {0}.
It is not much, but it gives the colony a place to pause, remember, and honor those who came before them.
[[C]][[+]]All beavers permanently gain the <GreenHighlight>+Remembrance</GreenHighlight> Wellbeing bonus.

No farewell is given for {0}. No marker is built. The colony simply returns to work.
Some beavers try not to think about it, but the silence settles heavily over the settlement. To them, it feels as if {0} was simply ignored.
[[C]][[-]]All beavers suffer Heavy Hearts effect for {0}d.

The colony returns to work.
There is sadness, but also understanding. Food must be gathered, water must be stored, and the settlement must survive. For now, the beavers carry the loss quietly and move on.
